title: Senior Public Relations Executive
description:
About Ruder Finn India:

Ruder Finn is one of the world’s largest independent communications agencies. Born in New York in 1948 with a restless ‘What’s Next’ spirit that still drives us today, it was one of the first PR agencies in the world. From bold ideas to breakthrough storytelling, we help brands, leaders, and causes stay ahead in a world that never stands still.

In India, our journey has been nothing short of electric. In just 7 years, we’ve scaled from a boutique agency to one of the fastest-growing large agencies in the country. Along the way, we’ve built a culture where people grow, experiment, and shine, proven by consistently leading the people and campaign award wins. With 250+ awards in the last 5 years and titles like Mid-Sized Agency of the Year and Large Agency of the Year (multiple t imes), we’re not just part of the conversation. We’re shaping it.




Why Ruder Finn India?

At Ruder Finn, we don't just do PR - we shape what's next. Here's why you'll love being part of our story:

• Tech at the core: Whether it's healthcare, consumer, or corporate, we bring a tech-first lens to everything we do. From RF.AIO to our Tech Lab and RF Studio, innovation isn't a department here, it's a mindset.

• Talent comes first: We believe in meritocracy and back it up by investing heavily in learning, development, and young leadership. If you've got the drive, you'll get the stage.

• Living "What's Next": Curiosity and hunger fuel us. We don't wait for trends; we build them. • Punching above our weight: From social-first storytelling for global icons like Meta and Adobe, to high-energy campaigns with LGP and MG - we play in big leagues with bold ideas.

• Work hard, play harder: Yes, we deliver for our clients, but we're equally passionate about telling our own story. #LifeAtRF is about creativity, camaraderie, and culture - and our people bring it alive every day. Check it out- https://www.instagram.com/ruderfinnindia/




Opportunity:

This is your chance to step into a role that’s as dynamic as the brands you’ll work with. You’ll lead bold campaigns, mentor a team of curious, high-energy talent, and drive ideas that don’t just get heard, they spark conversation. Think of it as a front-row seat to transformation: shaping strategies, championing digital-first storytelling, and making an impact that lasts. If you’re hungry for growth, ready to challenge the status quo, and excited to be a trusted advisor to senior leadership, this is where it happens.




What You’ll Do

Be the Client Whisperer:

Be the daily go-to for clients — anticipate needs, deliver quality, and build trust.

Turn Buzz into By-lines:

Own media relations, pitch with confidence, and secure high-impact placements.

Strategy meets Storytelling:

Help craft narratives that align business goals with audience relevance.

Lift as you Lead:

Guide and support Junior Executives; lead by example and lift as you climb.

Chase the Impact:

Measure success, report insights, and constantly push for better outcomes.




What We Expect from You:

• 2–3 years of PR/communications experience, ideally with agency exposure.

• Proven media relations skills and ability to handle multiple accounts especially GEC clients.

• Strong writing and storytelling capabilities.

• Initiative-driven, detail-oriented, and dependable under pressure.

• A collaborative team player with leadership potential and creative flair.

Indigo Group is a Mumbai-headquartered, diversified business conglomerate with a strong presence across Technology, Infrastructure, and Food Processing sectors. Under the visionary leadership of Chairman Mr. Vinit Kumar, Indigo Group has created significant value for its partners and associates over a span of more than two and a half decades.

title: Product Operations Coordinator
description:
Product Operations Coordinator — Release & Delivery

Product Strategy & Operations

Department: Product — Product Strategy & Operations

Reports To: Sr. Director, Product Strategy & Corporate Development

Location: Hybrid—Pune Must work US Central business hours (minimum 9am-1pm CT overlap)

Employment Type: Full-Time

Level: Junior to Mid-Level (2-4 years experience)




About the Role

We are looking for a Product Operations Coordinator to serve as the operational heartbeat of our Product organization.

This role owns the end-to-end release coordination cycle, launch readiness processes, and commercialization activities that ensure what our product teams build gets delivered to customers effectively. You will be the single owner of the recurring operational cadences that keep the Product org aligned: the Release Planning meeting, launch coordination, Community of Practice, and Product All Hands. You will also manage the artifacts that communicate product progress to the rest of the organization — including the roadmap, release notes, and roadmap presentations.

This is a role for someone who thrives on bringing order to complexity through proactive process improvement, who gets satisfaction from tracking every action item to completion, and who can facilitate meetings across diverse cross-functional stakeholders with confidence and clarity.

You will start by running established processes and progressively take on process improvement and framework development as you build organizational context.




Key Responsibilities

Release & Launch Coordination

Own the weekly Release Planning meeting end-to-end: agenda preparation, facilitation, follow-up action tracking, and accountability for item completeness across all product teams.
Proactively collaborate with Product Managers and Product Owners across the full product portfolio to ensure release planning items are populated, reviewed, and ready for discussion ahead of each meeting.
Track release item completeness across product teams; identify gaps early and drive accountability for timely updates in project management tools.
Own the Launch Planning meeting: agenda, facilitation, and coordination of cross-functional readiness activities across enablement, legal, finance, and marketing.
Manage the initiative tracking process, ensuring all active product initiatives are properly established, categorized, and allocated within the quarterly roadmap framework.

Commercialization Coordination

Own the task list for commercialization activities across the full product portfolio, ensuring execution of operational steps including coordination with enablement, legal, finance, and revenue operations.
Partner with Product Managers and Product Marketing to ensure all pre-launch operational requirements are identified, assigned, and tracked to completion.
Coordinate SKU launch readiness activities across business operations, legal, and finance stakeholders per the established commercialization workflow.

Product Team Operating Rhythm

Own the Community of Practice (CoP) meeting: coordinate topics with Product Managers and Product Leadership, identify speakers, manage the agenda, and ensure sessions deliver value.
Own the Product All Hands meeting: coordinate content, agenda, and logistics to keep the broader Product organization informed and aligned.

Cross-Functional Artifact Management

Maintain the externally-shared product roadmap, ensuring it reflects current priorities and timelines accurately.
Coordinate roadmap artifact updates (presentation decks) for use in cross-functional and executive discussions.
Manage the cross-functional release notes process, ensuring coordination between Product, Marketing, and other stakeholders on content and timing.




Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in related field or equivalent combination of education and experience.
2-4 years of experience in project management, program coordination, product operations, or a similar operational role within a technology or SaaS environment.
Demonstrated ability to facilitate cross-functional meetings with stakeholders from diverse functions (engineering, legal, finance, sales, marketing, customer success).
Strong organizational skills with meticulous attention to detail — you are the person who tracks every action item and follows up until it is done.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. You can distill complex project status into clear, actionable updates for multiple audiences.
Comfort working with project management and collaboration tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence, or similar Agile/project tracking platforms).
Ability to work US Central business hours with consistent availability for live meetings and real-time collaboration.
Self-starter who can operate with limited direction once processes and expectations are established.




Preferred Qualifications

Experience in B2B SaaS product operations or release management.
Familiarity with product commercialization or go-to-market coordination processes.
Experience with Atlassian tools (Jira, Confluence) for project tracking and documentation.
Exposure to insurance technology, financial services technology, or similar regulated industry software.
Experience coordinating across geographically distributed teams.
PMP, CAPM, or Agile certification (Scrum Master, SAFe, etc.).




Tools & Technologies

You will work daily with Jira and Confluence for project tracking and documentation, Salesforce for CRM context, Microsoft 365 (Teams, Outlook, PowerPoint, Excel) for communication and reporting, and Tableau/Snowflake for data access. Experience with AI productivity tools (e.g., Claude) is a plus.




What Success Looks Like

First 90 Days

Fully own the Release Planning meeting with no reliance on interim coverage.
Establish a repeatable release item completeness tracking mechanism that PMs/POs engage with consistently.
Take over CoP and All Hands meeting coordination with a documented forward-looking content plan.
Document the current release, launch, and commercialization processes as-is, identifying initial improvement opportunities.
Build working relationships with Product Managers and key cross-functional stakeholders across the full product organization.

6-12 Months

Deliver measurable improvements to the release planning framework (e.g., reduced cycle friction, improved item completeness rates).
Establish a documented commercialization coordination playbook that can be followed consistently across launches.
Confirm and execute the transition of Launch Planning ownership to Product Marketing, or establish long-term ownership within Product Operations.
Reduce the operational burden on Product Managers by proactively managing release/launch coordination activities they previously handled themselves.




About the Team

The Product Strategy & Operations team sits at the intersection of strategic portfolio management and operational execution within Zywave. We partner closely with Product Management, Engineering, and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure the Product organization can deliver value to customers efficiently.

title: Junior Cyber Security Analyst - Tier 1
description:
Technology is our how. And people are our why. For over two decades, we have been harnessing technology to drive meaningful change.



By combining world-class engineering, industry expertise and a people-centric mindset, we consult and partner with leading brands from various industries to create dynamic platforms and intelligent digital experiences that drive innovation and transform businesses.



From prototype to real-world impact - be part of a global shift by doing work that matters.





Job Description



The Tier 1 SOC Analyst is part of the front line of our Security Operations Center, responsible for continuous monitoring, initial investigation, and rapid response to security alerts and incidents. You will be acting as the first point of contact for detecting suspicious activity, escalating threats, and ensuring the environment remains secure.



Strong knowledge of security threats, incident detection technologies, and investigation techniques is expected. Candidates should demonstrate adaptability, willingness to work in 24x7 shifts, and eagerness to learn emerging security tools and techniques.



Responsibilities:




Monitor SOAR, SIEM, IDS/IPS, endpoint, and network security tools for alerts and anomalies.



Perform triage of incoming security events to assess severity, scope, and potential impact.



Investigate alerts to differentiate false positives from genuine threats.



Document findings clearly and escalate incidents according to SOC playbooks.



Initiate containment measures for confirmed incidents (e.g., isolating endpoints, disabling accounts).



Support vulnerability and patch management activities by monitoring for unpatched systems.



Maintain situational awareness of current threats and common attack techniques (e.g., phishing, malware, brute force).



Contribute to incident reports and post-incident reviews.



Use SOAR platforms for automating detection and response where possible.





Qualifications



Experience:




1-2 years of experience in Security Operations or in a SOC
Experience with SIEM, EDR, IAM, DLP, and incident response.
Familiarity with host-based detection and prevention suites (e.g., Microsoft Defender, Azure Security Center, Sentinel, Cisco Umbrella, M-Vision).
Familiarity with security incident management tools (e.g., ServiceNow).
Working knowledge of Microsoft and/or other OS environments.

Technical Skills:




Solid understanding of TCP/IP, firewalls, VPNs, IDS/IPS, anti-virus, and endpoint protection.
Basic understanding of firewall configuration and system patching.
Experience with log analysis and malware detection.
Familiarity with scripting languages (e.g., Python, Perl) is a plus.
Certifications such as Microsoft Security Certifications, Comptia Sec+/CYSA, ISC2 CC are a plus


Additional Information
